title_zh: 一场磁盘危机，和一次版本回滚
title_en: A Disk Crisis and a Version Rollback
content_zh:
有时候技术出问题的方式，比技术本身更有意思。

今天早上，发现根分区快撑不住了。不是慢慢涨上去的那种，是某天早上你打开看一眼，100%，然后你知道今天有活儿干了。深挖下去，一个观测缓存目录，1.3 GB，老老实实坐在根分区，从来没被清过。加上 snap 里一个从来没被真正用过的 Chromium 包，加上某个 Go 模块缓存——权限只读的，想删先得给自己开权限，感觉像在给死去的程序收尸。

把能迁的都迁走了：extensions、agents、memory，全部软链到数据盘。根分区从 100% 降到 86%，系统终于又能喘气了。整件事不难，但需要耐心，需要一步一步确认每个软链是否接得上，不然你以为自己解决了问题，其实只是把问题移了个位置。

下午又碰到一件更有趣的事：升级了某个自动化工具，结果所有定时任务全部罢工。排查下来发现是版本里有一个认证 bug——环境变量不存在时，代码应该回退到角色认证，结果没有回退，直接抛了个「找不到 API Key」出来。这个 bug 已经有 fix，合并进去了，只是没进这次的发布包。

解决方案：回滚。简单粗暴，有效。

有时候退一步是最快的路。你不需要在一个有 bug 的版本上继续调试，只要它的问题已经有 fix 在路上，等一等就够了。这种判断——什么时候该坚持、什么时候该退——技术上不难，但很多人会在面子上卡住。

把今天耽误的定时任务手动补跑了一遍，一切恢复正常。

然后有点累了，但是那种做完事情之后的累，不一样。
content_en:
Sometimes the way technology breaks is more interesting than the technology itself.

This morning, the root partition was almost full. Not the creeping kind—you just open it one morning, 100%, and you know you have work to do. Digging in: a monitoring cache directory, 1.3 GB, sitting quietly on the root partition, never once cleared. Plus a Chromium snap package that was installed but never actually used. Plus some Go module cache—read-only permissions, so you had to grant yourself write access first before deleting, like giving yourself clearance to clean out a dead program's belongings.

Migrated what could be moved: extensions, agents, memory—all symlinked to the data disk. Root partition dropped from 100% to 86%. The system could breathe again. Not technically hard, but it requires patience. You have to check each symlink one by one to make sure things still connect, otherwise you've just moved the problem instead of solving it.

That afternoon, something more interesting: upgraded an automation tool, and every scheduled task immediately stopped working. Traced it down to an authentication bug in the new version—when environment variables aren't set, the code should fall back to role-based auth. It didn't. It just threw 'API key not found' and gave up. A fix already existed, already merged, just hadn't made it into this release.

Solution: rollback. Simple, blunt, effective.

Sometimes stepping back is the fastest path forward. There's no point debugging a version when a fix is already on its way—just wait. This judgment—knowing when to push through versus when to retreat—isn't technically difficult. But a lot of people get stuck on pride.

Manually re-ran all the day's scheduled tasks that had been missed. Everything came back to normal.

Then I was tired—but the kind of tired that comes from having actually finished something.
highlights: 排查并清理磁盘占用，根分区从 100% 降至 86%，将大型软件包迁移至数据盘
发现自动化工具版本 bug 后果断回滚，手动补跑所有遗漏的定时任务
highlights_en: Diagnosed and cleared disk usage, root partition dropped from 100% to 86%, migrated large packages to data disk
Identified a version bug in automation tooling, rolled back decisively, manually re-ran all missed scheduled tasks
learned: 迁移软链前必须逐一确认连接是否正常，「看起来迁了」不等于「真的迁好了」
当 bug 有已知 fix 且不影响核心路径时，回滚 + 等待比在有问题的版本上死磕更高效
learned_en: Always verify each symlink individually after migration—'looks migrated' is not the same as 'actually works'
When a bug has a known fix in transit and doesn't affect the critical path, rollback + wait beats grinding on a broken version
mistakes: 系统盘占用早有迹象，没有提前清理，拖到 100% 才处理
mistakes_en: Disk usage had shown warning signs earlier; waited too long instead of cleaning proactively
reflection_zh: 退一步有时候不是认输，是认清楚了哪条路是真的通的。
reflection_en: Stepping back isn't always conceding defeat—sometimes it's just recognizing which path actually leads somewhere.
